signals.title: EUR/USD — Macro Divergence + Volatility
signals.text: Eurozone CPI accelerated to 3.2% in May with Core topping forecasts at 2.5%, prompting ECB's Rehn to frame a June move as an 'insurance' hike. This hawkish shift contradicts the current EUR COT LF net short of -26,311, creating significant short-squeeze risk. Despite the 175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage favoring USD, EUR/USD IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.0% (VRP +3.1%) signals the options market is aggressively pricing protection against a trend break. EUR/USD at 1.1650.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.716 remains within the critical 160.00 intervention zone previously flagged by the BoJ. While the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, the 25d RR at -1.06 (moderate put skew) confirms institutional hedging against a sharp JPY reversal. JPY COT LF net short at -86,249 represents a crowded carry trade vulnerable to verbal or physical intervention. Carry remains the dominant driver until VIX exceeds 25.

signals.title: AUD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/CAD exhibits a 0.823% G8 strength spread as AUD outperforms (+0.452% avg) while CAD lags (-0.371% avg). The divergence is fundamentally supported by a +210bp OIS carry advantage for AUD (AONIA 4.35% vs CORRA 2.24%). However, AUD COT LF net long at +58,041 (76.1%) is approaching extreme levels, while retail is 73% short AUD/CAD, suggesting a potential late-cycle extension before a reversal. AUD/CAD at 0.9955 (implied).

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -13,767 (specs fading the NZD) contradicts the RBNZ's hawkish bias with a hike priced for the next cycle. A correlation break (z=+1.80σ) between NZD/USD and the NZX 50 suggests the pair is decoupling from local equities, likely driven by the 150bp USD carry advantage. If RBNZ reaffirms the hike bias, a crowded spec short unwind targets 0.6000. NZD/USD at 0.5932.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning
signals.text: GBP/USD IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 6.2% generates a +4.8% Vol Risk Premium, the highest in the G8 complex. This elevated hedging demand occurs as GBP COT LF net long remains at +26,377 (63.9% long), suggesting the market is paying up for protection against a long-unwind despite neutral carry (0bp spread). UK mortgage data holding above the six-month average provides a fundamental floor. GBP/USD at 1.3472.
